rulings.Case Title: PHILLIP COLLINS et al VS. HAYLEE YEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday March 12, 2015, Line 2, Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Pro Tem Judge Dennis Hyde, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to March 23, 2015 at 1:30 p.m. in Department 301 before Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to hydelaw@pacbell.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STINSON, CHARLES, AKA CHUCK STINSON et al VS. PHYLLIS VOISENAT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default Taken Against Deceased Corss Defendant John Bonjean
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 12, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS STINSON, CHARLES, AKA CHUCK STINSON, AND MADELYN FOSSAT AS ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF JOHN MICHAEL BONJEAN, DECEASED'S Motion To Vacate Default Taken Against Deceased Cross Defendant John Bonjean. The motion is granted. Cross-complainant failed to comply with the requirements of CCP 377.40 et seq. Cross-complainant did not amend the cross-complaint in order to maintain the claim against decedent's personal representative. The clerk of the court should not have entered the default against decedent and it is void. The court vacates the default per CCP 473(d). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: STINSON, CHARLES, AKA CHUCK STINSON et al VS. PHYLLIS VOISENAT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjuciation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 12, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT PHYLLIS VOISENAT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjuciation. The court treats the motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication as a motion for judgment on the pleadings. See White v. County of Orange (1985) 166 Cal.App.3d 566, 569 (disagreed with on other grounds in John R. v. Oakland Unified School Dist. (1989) 48 Cal.3d 438). Defendant's motion largely addresses pleading defects that can be cured. The court grants the motion for judgment on the pleadings with 20 days leave to amend. The court vacates the existing trial date. Defendant can respond to the second amended complaint as she sees fit, including renewing her motion for summary judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JORDANNE HO-SHUE et al VS. GDL SFO, INC. DBA GOLD DUST LOUNGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Class Discovery Conference Pursuant To Crc, Rule 3.762
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday March 12, 2015, Line 5, Class Discovery Conference Pursuant To CRC, Rule 3.762. Pro Tem Judge Dennis Hyde, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to April 9, 2015. No timely courtesy copies were received in connection the motion. The "brief case summary and outline of anticipated discovery issues" ordered in connection with the scheduling of the hearing was apparently not timely filed, and no courtesy copy was received, five days before the hearing. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in an effort prepare a joint "brief case summary and outline of anticipated discovery issues" to be filed, and a courtesy copy lodged in Depart 302, together with courtesy copies of all other relevant documents, on or before March 27, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to hydelaw@pacbell.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TRULIA, INC. VS. MOVE SALES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 12, 2015, Line 11, Order To Show Cause Re: Plaintiff Trulia, Inc.'s Request for Preliminary Injunction. Preliminary injunction denied. Trulia, Inc. ("Trulia") has not established a likelihood of prevailing on the merits. Section 13 of the Platform Services Agreement ("Agreement") provides that Move Sales, Inc. ("MSI") may immediately terminate the Agreement if a Zillow parent or subsidiary acquires all or substantially all of its assets. The evidence establishes that although Trulia remained a freestanding corporate entity after the reverse triangular merger, its assets were acquired by a Zillow subsidiary because it became a Zillow subsidiary. Trulia has also not established irreparable harm if a preliminary injunction is not granted. It is undisputed that the listing data is available from other sources, and therefore the service provided by MSI is not "peculiar, extraordinary, or unique." (Kennedy v. Bank of America (1965) 237 Cal.App.2d 637, 647.) Further, the parties explicitly agreed that any breach could be compensated by $50,000, which is a strong indication that money damages are a sufficient remedy. (Agreement, Sec. 10.) Lastly, public policy supports businesses having the ability to freely enter into contracts. It follows that businesses should have the ability to freely terminate or breach contracts, provided that they are willing to pay damages for the breach. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
